tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Arrowroot is a tropical root vegetable known for its high starch content and smooth texture.
  • It is widely used as a natural thickening agent in soups, sauces, and desserts.
  • It is gluten-free and easy to digest, making it ideal for infants and people with digestive issues.
  • Arrowroot powder is commonly used in baking and traditional cooking.
  • It has a neutral flavor, allowing it to blend easily with various dishes.

tab2.title: Benefits
tab2.content:
• Supports digestion and gut health
  • Gluten-free cooking alternative
  • Provides quick energy
  • Helps regulate metabolism

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content:
• Often used as a substitute for cornstarch.
  • Popular in traditional herbal remedies.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Beetroot is a vibrant red root vegetable known for its natural sweetness and earthy flavor.
  • It is widely used in salads, juices, and cooked dishes.
  • It contains powerful antioxidants that support heart health.
  • Beetroot improves blood circulation and stamina.
  • It is also used as a natural coloring agent in foods.

tab2.title: Benefits
tab2.content:
• Improves blood flow
  • Supports heart health
  • Boosts stamina
  • Rich in antioxidants

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content:
• Leaves are also edible and nutritious.
  • Used in sports nutrition drinks.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Black radish is a root vegetable with dark skin and sharp, spicy flavor.
  • It is commonly used in traditional medicine.
  • It supports liver detoxification and digestion.
  • It is often eaten raw or pickled.
  • It has a strong aroma and pungent taste.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Carrot is a widely consumed root vegetable known for its bright orange color and sweet taste.
  • It is eaten raw, cooked, or juiced.
  • It is rich in beta-carotene, essential for eye health.
  • Carrots are versatile and used in many dishes worldwide.
  • They are crunchy and refreshing.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Cassava is a starchy tuber widely consumed in tropical regions.
  • It is a major source of carbohydrates.
  • Used to produce tapioca and flour.
  • Must be properly cooked to remove toxins.
  • It is a staple food in many countries.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Celeriac is a root vegetable related to celery.
  • It has a mild, nutty flavor.
  • Commonly used in soups and salads.
  • Low in calories and nutritious.
  • Popular in European cuisine.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Chinese potato is a small starchy tuber widely used in Asian and Indian cuisine.
  • It is often cooked in curries and stir-fries.
  • It has a mild flavor and firm texture.
  • It is highly nutritious and energy-rich.
  • Commonly known as Koorka in India.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Daikon is a long white radish commonly used in Asian cooking.
  • It has a mild, slightly sweet flavor.
  • Used in soups, salads, and pickles.
  • Helps digestion and detoxification.
  • Popular in Japanese and Korean cuisine.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Elephant yam is a large tropical tuber used in Indian cooking.
  • It has a dense texture and earthy flavor.
  • Used in curries and fried dishes.
  • It is rich in nutrients and fiber.
  • Known as Suran in India.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Galangal is a rhizome similar to ginger but with a sharper taste.
  • Used in Thai and Indonesian cuisine.
  • It adds a citrus-like flavor to dishes.
  • It has medicinal properties.
  • Used in soups and curries.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • Key ingredient in Thai Tom Yum soup.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Garlic is a strong-flavored bulb vegetable used worldwide.
  • It enhances flavor and aroma of food.
  • It has powerful medicinal properties.
  • Used in cooking and traditional medicine.
  • It has antibacterial effects.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Ginger is a widely used rhizome with a spicy flavor.
  • It is used in cooking, beverages, and medicine.
  • It aids digestion and relieves nausea.
  • It has anti-inflammatory properties.
  • Used globally in various cuisines.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Horseradish is a pungent root vegetable used as a condiment.
  • It has a very strong, spicy flavor.
  • Used in sauces and dips.
  • Popular in European cuisine.
  • Often paired with meat dishes.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Jerusalem artichoke is a tuber with a slightly sweet, nutty flavor.
  • Also called sunchoke.
  • Used in soups and roasted dishes.
  • Rich in dietary fiber.
  • Good for gut health.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Leek is a mild-flavored vegetable related to onion and garlic.
  • It has a subtle sweet taste.
  • Used in soups and stews.
  • Common in European dishes.
  • It is nutritious and low in calories.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Lotus root is the edible stem of the lotus plant.
  • It has a crunchy texture and mild taste.
  • Used in Asian cuisine.
  • It has a unique pattern when sliced.
  • It is rich in nutrients and fiber.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Onion is one of the most widely used vegetables across the world.
  • It has a strong aroma and slightly sweet flavor when cooked.
  • Used as a base ingredient in many dishes.
  • Available in red, white, and yellow varieties.
  • Essential in global cuisines.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Parsnip is a sweet root vegetable similar to carrot.
  • It has a creamy white color.
  • Used in soups and roasted dishes.
  • Becomes sweeter when cooked.
  • Popular in winter cuisine.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Potato is one of the most widely consumed tubers worldwide.
  • It is a staple food in many countries.
  • Used in a variety of dishes like fries, curries, and mashed preparations.
  • It has a mild taste and soft texture when cooked.
  • Highly versatile in cooking.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Purple carrot is an ancient variety of carrot.
  • It has a deep purple color due to anthocyanins.
  • It is sweeter and richer in antioxidants.
  • Used in salads and juices.
  • More nutritious than regular carrots.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Radish is a crunchy root vegetable with a slightly spicy taste.
  • It is commonly eaten raw in salads.
  • Used in various cuisines worldwide.
  • Helps in digestion.
  • Available in different shapes and colors.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Rutabaga is a root vegetable similar to turnip.
  • It has a slightly sweet flavor.
  • Used in soups and mashed dishes.
  • It is hardy and grows in cold climates.
  • Nutritious and filling.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Salsify is a root vegetable with a mild oyster-like flavor.
  • It is used in soups and stews.
  • It has a long, thin root.
  • Popular in European cuisine.
  • It is nutritious and unique in taste.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Scorzonera is a root vegetable with black skin and white flesh.
  • It has a mild, sweet taste.
  • Used in soups and roasted dishes.
  • Similar to salsify.
  • Rich in nutrients.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Shallot is a small bulb vegetable related to onion.
  • It has a milder and sweeter flavor.
  • Used in gourmet cooking.
  • Common in sauces and dressings.
  • Popular in French cuisine.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Sweet potato is a starchy root vegetable with natural sweetness.
  • It is highly nutritious and rich in vitamins.
  • Used in both sweet and savory dishes.
  • Available in different colors.
  • Popular worldwide.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Taro is a tropical root vegetable used in many cuisines.
  • It has a starchy texture.
  • Used in curries and desserts.
  • Requires cooking before eating.
  • Common in Asian cooking.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Turmeric root is a bright yellow rhizome widely used in cooking and medicine.
  • It has anti-inflammatory properties.
  • Used in curries and herbal remedies.
  • Known for its strong color and flavor.
  • Essential in Indian cuisine.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Turnip is a root vegetable with a slightly bitter taste.
  • Used in soups and curries.
  • It is nutritious and low in calories.
  • Leaves are also edible.
  • Common in cold climates.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Wasabi root is a spicy rhizome used in Japanese cuisine.
  • It has a strong, pungent flavor.
  • Commonly served with sushi.
  • It clears nasal passages.
  • Rare and expensive.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • Real wasabi is rare; most is imitation.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• White radish is a mild-flavored root vegetable.
  • Used in salads and cooking.
  • Popular in Asian cuisine.
  • Helps digestion.
  • Refreshing taste.

tab1.title: Details
tab1.content:
• Yam is a starchy tuber widely consumed in Africa and Asia.
  • It is a staple food.
  • Used in various traditional dishes.
  • Rich in energy.
  • Different from sweet potato.
